rules.description: This rule is helpful with card testing. It will block charges if an IP address was successfully authorized on your account more than one time.
If you want to be more aggressive in preventing card testing, use this rule in conjunction with :total_charges_per_ip_address_hourly:
This rule is helpful with card cashing. It will block charges if a card number was successfully authorized on your account in the past hour more than one time.
If you want to be more aggressive in preventing card cashing, use this rule in conjunction with :total_charges_per_card_number_hourly:

Make sure you're collecting zip codes in your checkout form to use this rule. This rule will block charges if the card issuer can't validate the zip code provided with what they have on file for the card.

Make sure you're collecting CVC in your checkout form to use this rule. This rule will block charges if the card issuer can't validate the CVC (or CVV) code provided with what they have on file for the card.

This rule will place any orders with items from the specified categories into the Manual Review list on your Stripe Dashboard so that you can take a second look. Note that these payments are still processed and the customer charged unless you manually cancel the order.

Assume that you sell two products ('Trial class' and '10 class package') and you send Stripe the product name as the charge description. This rule will place any orders where the charge description is exactly 'Trial class' in the Manual Review list on your Stripe Dashboard so that you can take a second look. Note that these payments are still processed and the customer charged unless you manually cancel the order.
This catches fraudulent actors who compromise accounts and rapidly test multiple email addresses across different payment methods.

Flags SEPA transactions with mismatched shipping/billing countries and express shipping, targeting fraudulent actors who exploit longer settlement windows to receive goods before payment failures are detected. Note that you would need to pass metadata indicating the shipping speed.

Prevents high-value electronics purchases through BNPL methods, which are commonly targeted by fraudulent actors who receive goods after the first installment but never complete payments. Note that you would need to pass metadata to indicate the product category.
The default behavior for Radar for Fraud Teams and Radar for Platforms is to place payments into review that we suspect have an elevated risk of fraud.

rules.rule: CVC verification fails based on risk score
rules.description: Enable this rule to block payments that fail a card issuer’s CVC verification check, unless Stripe evaluates it as low risk. This rule doesn’t block payments where the customer doesn’t provide the CVC number because they, for example, use a wallet, or their card issuer doesn’t support its verification.

rules.rule: Postal code verification fails based on risk score
rules.description: Enable this rule to block payments that fail a card issuer’s postal code verification check, unless Stripe evaluates it as low risk. This rule doesn’t block payments where the customer doesn’t provide the postal code, or their card issuer doesn’t support its verification.

You can also use attributes as a corresponding value within a condition. For example, you can create a rule to block payments where the issuing country of the card doesn’t match the country where the payment took place.
